subtopics.title: Feature Scaling
subtopics.explanation: Scaling features to a similar range ensures that no single feature dominates due to its magnitude. Essential for distance-based algorithms (kNN, SVM) and gradient descent optimization.
subtopics.example: Without scaling, a feature ranging 0-1000 (e.g., income) would dominate a feature ranging 0-10 (e.g., years of experience) in distance calculations.
subtopics.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
features = df[['income', 'age', 'experience']]
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_features = scaler.fit_transform(features)

subtopics.title: Encoding
subtopics.explanation: Converting categorical text data into numerical format for machine learning models. One-hot encoding creates binary columns for each category, while label encoding assigns integer labels.
subtopics.example: One-hot encoding 'color': red, blue, green becomes three columns: is_red, is_blue, is_green.

subtopics.title: Feature Selection
subtopics.explanation: Identifying and retaining only the most relevant features to reduce dimensionality, improve model performance, and reduce overfitting. Methods include correlation analysis, recursive feature elimination, and feature importance from tree models.
subtopics.example: In a house price model, features like 'location' and 'sqft' are kept, while 'street_name' might be irrelevant.
subtopics.code:
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
model = RandomForestClassifier()
model.fit(X, y)
importances = model.feature_importances_
important_features = X.columns[importances > 0.05]
